name: Ahuimanu
desc: Deep Kaneohe valley — rural feel, lush greenery, affordable, longer commute, tight-knit local community.
insider: The community is very tight-knit — introduce yourself to neighbors early, it goes a long way
Wild chickens and roosters crow starting around 4am — light sleepers should invest in earplugs or a white noise machine
Valley thunderstorms roll in fast in the afternoon — always have a rain jacket in the car
The deeper you go into the valley, the more rural and peaceful it becomes — back roads feel like old Hawaii
Haleiwa Joe's at Haiku Gardens is the closest quality sit-down restaurant — great prime rib with Ko'olau cliff views
Ahuimanu Stream floods quickly in heavy rain — avoid the low sections of Ahuimanu Road when storms roll in
Neighbors share avocado, mango, and breadfruit freely in season — bring something to trade and you'll never run out of fruit
Commute through Likelike Highway tunnel to downtown is 20-25 min without traffic but 45+ during morning rush hour
Senator Hiram Fong's old plantation estate nearby had gorgeous botanical gardens — the valley retains that lush, cultivated character
Haiku Road bottlenecks badly at school drop-off times — leave before 7am or after 8:30am to avoid the crawl
